title: Fix enum range constraints missing for rand variables in sub-objects
body:
## Summary

When a class contains a `rand` sub-object (e.g., `rand InnerObj inner`) whose members include enum-typed `rand` variables, `randomize()` produces values outside the valid enum range. Per IEEE 1800-2017 §18.4, enum variables should be constrained to their declared members during randomization.

## Current Behavior

- `rand color_e color` inside a sub-object (`rand InnerObj inner`) can produce values not in the enum (e.g., `2'd3` for a 3-member enum with values 0, 1, 2)
- Direct enum members of the top-level class are correctly constrained
- The issue only affects enum variables accessed through sub-object composition (`obj.inner.color`)

## Expected Behavior

- Enum-typed `rand` variables inside sub-objects should only take values matching declared enum members, same as direct class members
- `randomize()` should never produce out-of-range enum values regardless of object nesting depth

## Source

- planvtech/PlanV_Verilator_Feature_Tests#83

---
PR is coming.
